title: Composition of bijective functions is bijective
informal_statement: If f and g are bijective functions, then g composed with f (g after f) is bijective.
informal_proof_idea: Bijective means injective and surjective. Combine the injective-composition and surjective-composition facts (already known for each half separately) into the paired Bijective witness.
formal_statement_pp: theorem bijective_comp (α β γ : Type) (f : α → β) (g : β → γ) (hf : Function.Bijective f) (hg : Function.Bijective g) : Function.Bijective (g ∘ f)

review.review_basis: repo self-review; kernel-verified through the tracked proof-search loop

notes: Proof authority: Lean kernel via the tracked proof-search loop (episode 0b5fbe31), kernel_verified on the first attempt. This domain has `injective_comp` and `surjective_comp` separately but nothing combining them into the paired `Bijective` statement -- confirmed via `grep -rl Bijective packets/elementary/functions/*.json` (only `id_bijective` hit, nothing about composition) before authoring. Proved via the anonymous-constructor pairing of `Function.Injective.comp`/`Function.Surjective.comp` applied to each half of the two `Bijective` hypotheses, rather than searching for a possibly-differently-named `Function.Bijective.comp` convenience lemma (recent cycles' repeated experience with `mathlib_search_declarations` returning noisy generic 'comp' matches made deriving directly from the definition the faster, safer bet).
